author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
Thats your assumption and that might be true for some of those advocating for 40/60 or less but certainly not for all. I spend a great amount of time marketing my licenses so far:

Twitter
Reddit
TikTok
Build 2 dedicated TG groups
A custom signup form
Several Email templates to engage with the ULOs, depeding where they are from
Spend hours on making a visualy appealing onboarding guide
Provided countless hours of 1on1 support for my ULOs
started a private referral system
and so on.

While I was doing so I managed to get 14 licenses leased and activated. So taht around 3 hours per activated license so far. Meanwhile, lots of people have gotten licenses taken from the marketplace. I personally havent gotten one taken from there. However, I am still advocating for 30/70 because I think thats a fair split. I bought into Unity not only for my own personal financial gain, but also to change lives for my ULOs. And with 30% I feel I still get enough to have a comfortable live. But thats MY POV and everyone is free to have a differing opinion.

Regarding the spllit: Even with a split of 50/50 or 40/60 UNOs are still incentivized to do marketing if they want. BTW: You could even make an argument that UNOs who want a higher share should convince theier community/ULOs by offering a superior service compared to those who just put their licenses on the in-app marketplace. Also, I think a GOOD REFERRAL SYSTEM would be the most powerful marketing instrument, alongside sufficient revenue. And that revenue is achieved more easily with a higher ULO split 🤷

Everyone will have a different opinion on this and I dont think we will find a solution that fits all. So thats why we have this poll (which isnt a vote, but okay) and I will accept the outcome. I probably wont like it, but I will accept it, because thats what grown ups should do 😃

author: CloverNodes
msg: Thank you for your counterpoint and no I am not suggesting that everyone must become anything. Unity as a whole has gotten to where it is because of the success UNOs have brought to this project. There are countless people in this chat who have done an incredible amount of work to onboard ULOs and that is what I am advocating for with this minimum. I want to see the people who put in the work win. Now to answer your other question about people who have full time jobs and cannot really take the time to market, this is exactly why a higher minimum can benefit them too. They can simply list their licenses at a higher split and earn more once rewards and demand increase. But for that demand to increase we need ULOs and I am sure you agree they are the driving force here. In order for us to reach more ULOs we need word of mouth and we need a push from UNOs. So no, I am not suggesting everyone go full time on World Mobile, Minutes Network, or even Unity. I am saying the people who want to put in the work need to be incentivized the most and I firmly believe those same people will be the main drivers of growth in this initial phase. The rest who want to set it and forget it will have to wait or find another way to onboard ULOs. This is a sharing economy after all, work needs to be done in order for this to work.

author: Matej
msg:
This is not how serious projects are governed.

If the vote was publicly presented as __final__ and explicitly stated that it __will be adopted__, reopening it afterwards fundamentally undermines trust, credibility, and the legitimacy of any future governance process. A vote cannot be “final” only when the outcome is convenient.

Concerns and objections should have been raised **before** the poll, not after the community invested time, effort, and good faith into constructive discussion and decision-making. Changing the rules post-vote signals indecisiveness and poor leadership, and it sends a clear message that community input is conditional rather than respected.

If results can be disregarded or re-voted whenever they are uncomfortable, then the process itself becomes meaningless. Governance by polls only works if outcomes are honored.

The realization that a Telegram poll was not the appropriate mechanism should have been made __before__ declaring the vote final, not after the outcome was already established. 

And despite all, keep in mind that only verified UN owners participated in the poll.

I strongly urge the team to respect the original vote and adopt the agreed 60/40 split as stated. Anything else sets a dangerous precedent and seriously damages confidence in the project’s decision-making framework.
